item.title: Registered Nurse, ICU, Full-time Days, Clarksville at Vanderbilt University Medical Center
item.guid: Vanderbilt University Medical Center-Registered Nurse, ICU, Full-time Days, Clarksville-Clarksville, TN, US
item.description:
📍 Location: Clarksville, TN, US 💼 Type: Fulltime 🏠 Work: On-site
📝 Description: **Discover Vanderbilt University Medical Center** : Located in Nashville, Tennessee, and operating at a global crossroads of teaching, discovery, and patient care, VUMC is a community of individuals who come to work each day with the simple aim of changing the world. It is a place where your expertise will be valued, your knowledge expanded, and your abilities challenged. Vanderbilt Health is committed to an environment where everyone has the chance to thrive and where your uniqueness is sought and celebrated. It is a place where employees know they are part of something that is bigger than themselves, take exceptional pride in their work and never settle for what was good enough yesterday. Vanderbilt’s mission is to advance health and wellness through preeminent programs in patient care, education, and research.
**Organization:**
VCH Intensive Care Unit **Job Summary:**
Responsible for planning, organizing, and administering patient care within the scope of his/her training and expertise, collaborating with other professional disciplines to ensure effective and efficient patient care delivery and achievement of desired patient outcomes is met. This is done in conjunction with the objectives and policies of the hospital and the Nurse Practice Act of the State of Tennessee. .
Hours of Position:
Full\\-time (36 hours/week), Days (6:45am\\-6:45pm)
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
* Collaborates in establishing the plan of care, coordinates and implements care delivery while anticipating patient care needs across the continuum. * Analyzes comprehensive information pertinent to the patient's care and management to determine nursing diagnosis. * Continually utilizes the nursing process to evaluate patient's progress towards goals and applies appropriate interventions to ensure effective outcomes. * Employs strategies to promote health and a safe and healthy environment. The responsibilities listed are a general overview of the position and additional duties may be assigned. *
TECHNICAL CAPABILITIES
* NURSING PATIENT ASSESSMENT \\& EVALUATION HOME CARE(NOVICE): Possesses ample fundamental proficiency to successfully demonstrate the ability to assess and evaluate patients in practical applications of moderate difficulty in the home setting. The RN Home Care works autonomously to provide safe, thorough and effective physician\\-ordered care in the home setting. Conducts primary care patient interviews, assesses the safety of the home environment and performs a comprehensive physical assessment. The RN Home Care demonstrates appropriate acumen to recognize problems, ask questions and to contact the patient's primary care provider. * Nursing Patient Education (Novice): Possesses sufficient fundamental proficiency in providing patient education in practical applications of moderate difficulty. * Care Planning (Novice): Demonstrates ability to anticipate resource needs, identify distinct tasks, set priorities, schedule activities, meet deadlines, and organize work within areas of direct responsibility. Competently handles multiple assignments often simultaneously by prioritizing work into manageable and measurable units. Ability to adapt to changes to patient care and area needs with minimal disruption and loss of productivity. Follows up to assure problems and issues are resolved. * Evidence\\-Based Nutrition Practice (Novice): Possesses sufficient fundamental proficiency to successfully search for nutrition evidence to answer clinical questions. Generally works under the direction of others while accomplishing assignments.
**Our Nursing Philosophy:**
We believe highly skilled and specialized nursing care is essential to Vanderbilt University Medical Center’s mission of quality in patient care, education and research. We believe nursing is an applied art and science focused on helping people, families and communities reach excellent health and well\\-being.
As a Vanderbilt University Medical Center employee, you make a difference to our patients and their families by bringing compassion and care to those in need of hope and healing. Please see our current employee benefits offered:
* Affordable High Quality Health Plan Options * Dental and /or vision plan * 403 (b) retirement plan * Paid Time off (flex PTO) * Tuition Reimbursement and adoption assistance (maximums applied) * Short\\-Long term disability * Subsidized backup childcare * And many more... **Ask us about our current inpatient nursing supplemental Pay Program!**
**Achieve the Remarkable:**
Learn more about VUMC Nursing here .
**Core Accountabilities:**
Organizational Impact: Executes job responsibilities with the understanding of how output would affect and impact other areas related to own job area/team with occasional guidance. Problem Solving/ Complexity of work: Analyzes moderately complex problems using technical experience and judgment. Breadth of Knowledge: Has expanded knowledge gained through experience within a professional area. Team Interaction: Provides informal guidance and support to team members. **Core Capabilities** **:**
Supporting Colleagues:\\- Develops Self and Others: Invests time, energy, and enthusiasm in developing self/others to help improve performance e and gain knowledge in new areas.\\- Builds and Maintains Relationships: Maintains regular contact with key colleagues and stakeholders using formal and informal opportunities to expand and strengthen relationships.\\- Communicates Effectively: Recognizes group interactions and modifies one's own communication style to suit different situations and audiences. Delivering Excellent Services:\\- Serves Others with Compassion: Seeks to understand current and future needs of relevant stakeholders and customizes services to better address them.\\- Solves Complex Problems: Approaches problems from different angles; Identifies new possibilities to interpret opportunities and develop concrete solutions.\\- Offers Meaningful Advice and Support: Provides ongoing support and coaching in a constructive manner to increase employees' effectiveness. Ensuring High Quality: \\- Performs Excellent Work: Engages regularly in formal and informal dialogue about quality; directly addresses quality issues promptly.\\- Ensures Continuous Improvement: Applies various learning experiences by looking beyond symptoms to uncover underlying causes of problems and identifies ways to resolve them. \\- Fulfills Safety and Regulatory Requirements: Understands all aspects of providing a safe environment and performs routine safety checks to prevent safety hazards from occurring. Managing Resources Effectively: \\- Demonstrates Accountability: Demonstrates a sense of ownership, focusing on and driving critical issues to closure.\\- Stewards Organizational Resources: Applies understanding of the departmental work to effectively manage resources for a department/area.\\- Makes Data Driven Decisions: Demonstrates strong understanding of the information or data to identify and elevate opportunities. Fostering Innovation:\\- Generates New Ideas: Proactively identifies new ideas/opportunities from multiple sources or methods to improve processes beyond conventional approaches.\\- Applies Technology: Demonstrates an enthusiasm for learning new technologies, tools, and procedures to address short\\-term challenges.\\- Adapts to Change: Views difficult situations and/or problems as opportunities for improvement; actively embraces change instead of emphasizing negative elements. **Position Qualifications:**
**Responsibilities:**
**Certifications:**
LIC\\-Registered Nurse \\- Licensure\\-Others **Work Experience:**
Relevant Work Experience
**Experience Level:**
Less than 1 year **Education:**

📝 Description: **Discover Vanderbilt University Medical Center** : Located in Nashville, Tennessee, and operating at a global crossroads of teaching, discovery, and patient care, VUMC is a community of individuals who come to work each day with the simple aim of changing the world. **Organization:**
Vanderbilt Ortho Franklin\\- Lic **Job Summary:**
Supports the licensed healthcare team members in the provision of patient care to include direct patient care, providing healthcare information to patients/families, and following established plan of care. Works in close collaboration with the RN or a licensed healthcare team member to evaluate the patient's plan of care and progress toward goal attainment. All patient care activities are undertaken with the delegation/supervision of a licensed professional in accordance with VUMC standards and licensure regulations. .
**SHIFT:** Monday/Tuesday \\& Thursday Friday 7:00 a.m. \\- 5:30 p.m. (off on Wednesday) No holidays/weekends/on call
**Key Responsibilities:**
* Collaborates with patients/families and health care providers in the delivery of health care services. * Partners with the healthcare team by following established plan of care to ensure patient progress for effective outcomes. * Support plans to promote health and a safe and healthy environment. * The responsibilities listed are a general overview of the position and additional duties may be assigned.
**Technical Capabilities:**
* Clinical Patient Documentation (Novice): Possesses sufficient fundamental proficiency to successfully demonstrate patient documentation in practical applications of moderate difficulty. Generally works under the direction of others while accomplishing assignments. Contributes to the nursing assessment by collecting, reporting, and recording objective and subjective data in an accurate and timely manner. * LPN Patient Education (Novice): Possesses sufficient fundamental proficiency to successfully demonstrate patient education in practical applications of moderate difficulty. Generally works under the direction of others while accomplishing assignments. * LPN Care Planning (Novice): Participates in the development of the plan of care in consultation with the licensed healthcare team. Demonstrates ability to anticipate resource needs, identify distinct tasks, set priorities, schedule activities, meet deadlines, and organize work within areas of direct responsibility. Competently handles multiple assignments often simultaneously, prioritizing work into manageable and measurable units. Ability to adapt to changes to patient care and area needs with minimal disruption and loss of productivity. Follows up to assure problems and issues are resolved. * Clinical Competency (Novice): Possesses sufficient fundamental proficiency to successfully demonstrate nursing interventions (such as wound care, point of care testing, vitals signs, etc.) in practical applications of moderate difficulty. Generally works under the direction of others while accomplishing assignments. **Our Nursing Philosophy:**
We believe highly skilled and specialized nursing care is essential to Vanderbilt University Medical Center’s mission of quality in patient care, education and research. We believe nursing is an applied art and science focused on helping people, families and communities reach excellent health and well\\-being.
As a Vanderbilt University Medical Center employee, you make a difference to our patients and their families by bringing compassion and care to those in need of hope and healing. Please see our current employee benefits offered:
* Affordable High Quality Health Plan Options * Dental and /or vision plan * 403 (b) retirement plan * Paid Time off (flex PTO) * Tuition Reimbursement and adoption assistance (maximums applied) * Short\\-Long term disability * Subsidized backup childcare * And many more... **Ask us about our current inpatient nursing supplemental Pay Program!**
**Achieve the Remarkable:**
Learn more about VUMC Nursing here .
**Core Accountabilities:**
\\* Organizational Impact: Performs tasks that are typically routine that may impact team's performance with occasional guidance. \\* Problem Solving/ Complexity of work: Utilizes some discretion and research to solve routine problems. \\* Breadth of Knowledge: Applies knowledge of standards, established processes and procedure that apply to your own job. \\* Team Interaction: Provides guidance to entry level co\\-workers. **Core Capabilities** **:**
Supporting Colleagues : Develops Self and Others: Continuously improves own skills by identifying development opportunities.\\- Builds and Maintains Relationships: Seeks to understand colleagues' priorities, working styles and develops relationships across areas.\\- Communicates Effectively: Openly shares information with others and communicates in a clear and courteous manner. Applies the nursing process to plan and implement the care of patients, including patient and family education and continuum of care planning. Effectively communicates pertinent patient/family information to the health care team. Collaborates in establishing patient goals and evaluates progress to ensure effective outcomes. Furthers professional growth by engaging in learning activities according to the Board of Nursing standards. .
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
* Collaborates in establishing the plan of care, coordinates and implements care delivery while anticipating patient care needs across the continuum. * Analyzes comprehensive information pertinent to the patient's care and management to determine nursing diagnosis. * Continually utilizes the nursing process to evaluate patient's progress towards goals and applies appropriate interventions to ensure effective outcomes. * Employs strategies to promote health and a safe and healthy environment. The responsibilities listed are a general overview of the position and additional duties may be assigned. *
TECHNICAL CAPABILITIES
* Care Planning (Novice): Demonstrates ability to anticipate resource needs, identify distinct tasks, set priorities, schedule activities, meet deadlines, and organize work within areas of direct responsibility. Competently handles multiple assignments often simultaneously by prioritizing work into manageable and measurable units. Ability to adapt to changes to patient care and area needs with minimal disruption and loss of productivity. Follows up to assure problems and issues are resolved. * Nursing Patient Education (Novice): Possesses sufficient fundamental proficiency in providing patient education in practical applications of moderate difficulty. * Nursing Patient Assessment \\& Evaluation (Novice): Possesses sufficient fundamental proficiency to successfully demonstrate the ability to assess and evaluate patients in practical applications of moderate difficulty. Conducts primary care patient interviews and physical examinations. Demonstrates sufficient acumen to recognize problems, ask questions and to contact the patient's primary care provider and other members of the healthcare team. Has experience in gathering pertinent clinical data that aid in referral, treatment, or other primary care pathways. * Evidence\\-Based Practice (Novice): Possesses sufficient fundamental proficiency to successfully search for evidence to answer clinical questions. Generally works under the direction of others while accomplishing assignments.

We believe highly skilled and specialized nursing care is essential to Vanderbilt University Medical Center’s mission of quality in patient care, education and research. Breadth of Knowledge: Has expanded knowledge gained through experience within a professional area. Team Interaction: Provides informal guidance and support to team members. **Core Capabilities** **:**

Delivering Excellent Services:\\- Serves Others with Compassion: Seeks to understand current and future needs of relevant stakeholders and customizes services to better address them.\\- Solves Complex Problems: Approaches problems from different angles; Identifies new possibilities to interpret opportunities and develop concrete solutions.\\- Offers Meaningful Advice and Support: Provides ongoing support and coaching in a constructive manner to increase employees' effectiveness. **Organization:**
Nursing Informatics Teams **Job Summary:**
The PRN Nursing Informatics Specialist supports the integration of clinical practice and technology across the organization. This role provides on\\-demand support for system enhancements, workflow optimization, and technology implementation. Ideal candidates will be Registered Nurses with clinical experience, strong communication abilities, quick adoption of new skills, and a flexible schedule to assist during system upgrades, go\\-lives, or staff education needs. Able to cover daytime, evening, night, or weekend shifts, if needed. Role requires experience with Epic nursing documentation and technologies utilized at Vanderbilt University Medical Center.
(Note: This role is expected to work around 30 hours per week and be on\\-site the majority of time) .
**KEY RESPONSIBILITIES**
* Serves as an on\\-site liaison between clinical end\\-users and the IT team during system upgrades, enhancements, and technology implementations. * Utilizes current evidences based nursing knowledge, including research findings to guide practice. * Assists with gathering and communicating relevant data to define issues, identify problems, and engage stakeholders during implementation periods. * Supports area\\-specific workflows. Coaches customers thru processes to troubleshoot and resolve technological issues in collaboration with other teams as appropriate. * Supports workflow optimization by identifying gaps and contributing to current\\-state and future\\-state planning in collaboration with informatics and operational teams. * The responsibilities listed are a general overview of the position and additional duties may be assigned.
**REQUIREMENTS:**
* **EPIC experience** (Required) * Experience working in our VUMC environment (strongly preferred) * **Registered Nurse \\- Licensure** * Nursing Informatics Certifications or MSN in nursing informatics (a plus). * Have the ability to work on\\-site around **30 hours per week** .
**TECHNICAL CAPABILITIES**

Can explain basic product features and benefits using appropriate terminology. * Problem Management (Novice): Demonstrates foundational skills in incident tracking and issue documentation. Coordinates with informatics and IT teams to ensure smooth transitions and user readiness.

We are excited to have the opportunity to serve our customers across the entities at Vanderbilt University Medical Center in Inpatient, Emergency, Ambulatory, and Perioperative clinical spaces.
Our professional administrative functions include critical supporting roles in information technology and informatics, finance, administration, legal and community affairs, human resources, communications and marketing, development, facilities, and many more.

* Collaborates with patients/families and health care providers in the delivery of health care services. * Partners with the healthcare team by following established plan of care to ensure patient progress for effective outcomes. * Support plans to promote health and a safe and healthy environment. * The responsibilities listed are a general overview of the position and additional duties may be assigned.
**Technical Capabilities:**
* Clinical Patient Documentation (Novice): Possesses sufficient fundamental proficiency to successfully demonstrate patient documentation in practical applications of moderate difficulty. Generally works under the direction of others while accomplishing assignments. Contributes to the nursing assessment by collecting, reporting, and recording objective and subjective data in an accurate and timely manner. * LPN Patient Education (Novice): Possesses sufficient fundamental proficiency to successfully demonstrate patient education in practical applications of moderate difficulty. Generally works under the direction of others while accomplishing assignments. * LPN Care Planning (Novice): Participates in the development of the plan of care in consultation with the licensed healthcare team. Demonstrates ability to anticipate resource needs, identify distinct tasks, set priorities, schedule activities, meet deadlines, and organize work within areas of direct responsibility. Competently handles multiple assignments often simultaneously, prioritizing work into manageable and measurable units. Ability to adapt to changes to patient care and area needs with minimal disruption and loss of productivity. Follows up to assure problems and issues are resolved. * Clinical Competency (Novice): Possesses sufficient fundamental proficiency to successfully demonstrate nursing interventions (such as wound care, point of care testing, vitals signs, etc.) in practical applications of moderate difficulty. Generally works under the direction of others while accomplishing assignments. **Our Nursing Philosophy:**
We believe highly skilled and specialized nursing care is essential to Vanderbilt University Medical Center’s mission of quality in patient care, education and research. We believe nursing is an applied art and science focused on helping people, families and communities reach excellent health and well\\-being.

📝 Description: **Discover Vanderbilt University Medical Center** : Located in Nashville, Tennessee, and operating at a global crossroads of teaching, discovery, and patient care, VUMC is a community of individuals who come to work each day with the simple aim of changing the world. **Key Responsibilities:**
* Collaborates with patients/families and health care providers in the delivery of health care services * Partners with the healthcare team by following established plan of care to ensure patient progress for effective outcomes * Support plans to promote health and a safe and healthy environment * The responsibilities listed are a general overview of the position and additional duties may be assigned * Navigates intense medical scenarios with autonomy, under the direction of the supervising RN while maintaining compliance with the physician ordered plan of care * Administer intravenous fluids and perform sterile procedures * Recognize and respond to clinical situations appropriately and timely to prevent patient decompensation and injury * Identify and assess patient needs and clinical changes, accurately reporting assessment findings in a timely manner to Supervising RN and physician

item.title: RN II-ICU-CVICU – Medical ICU 1: San Luis Obispo, CA at HiredFirst
item.guid: HiredFirst-RN II-ICU-CVICU – Medical ICU 1: San Luis Obispo, CA-San Luis Obispo, CA, US
item.description:
📍 Location: San Luis Obispo, CA, US 💼 Type: Contract 🏠 Work: On-site 💰 Salary: USD 2,600 - 2,900 (weekly)
📝 Description: **RN II\\-ICU\\-CVICU\\-Medical ICU 1** ====================================
Location: San Luis Obispo, CA
Assignment Length: 13 Weeks (with option to extend)
* Shift: Night Shift 3 x 12s or 4 x 12s; rotating shifts (no blocked schedules), 1900\\-0730; Need TNCC, ACLS, BLS, NIHSS; Medical ICU/Trauma ICU, Neuro ICU. Call is rare. (2nd 12h)
Overview
We are seeking a Travel RN II\\-ICU\\-CVICU to join a healthcare team for a travel assignment in San Luis Obispo, CA. This opportunity allows you to work in a collaborative clinical environment while delivering high\\-quality patient care.
Assignment Details
* 13\\-week travel contract * Work with an experienced multidisciplinary healthcare team * Supportive clinical environment * Opportunity to expand specialty experience
Key Responsibilities
* Nestled on the Central California Coast, Sierra Vista has been providing care to our community since 1959 * Our 162\\-bed acute care facility includes a Level III Neonatal Intensive Care Unit and county designated trauma center * San Luis Obispo offers the excitement of a lively community while being a fifteen\\-minute drive from the serenity of Avila Beach, known for their natural hot springs, and Pismo Beach, known for their sand dunes and eucalyptus trees * Featuring a charming downtown, comfortable coastal weather, idyllic views, and an active lifestyle San Luis Obispo offers much to be had * Job Summary: Responsible for the planning, coordination, and provision of nursing care to patients on the critical care setting * Supervises and delegates care responsibilities to ancillary personnel commensurate with their scope of practice * Responsible for critical care nursing knowledge and skills applicable to but not limited to the care of patients with multi system failure requiring ventilation, neuro / trauma, respiratory failure, and hemodynamic monitoring * This position is represented by CNA * Job Requirements: Education and Work Experience: High School Education/GED or equivalent: Required Associate Degree(A.A * or A.S.): Required Bachelor's Degree in Nursing: Preferred Passing completion of a Critical Care Nursing course: Required Six months previous critical care experience: Preferred Licenses/Certifications: Current RN license in state of practice: Required Basic Life Support (BLS OR HS\\-BLS OR RQIBLS) certification: Required ACLS (Advanced Cardiovascular Life Support): Required Trauma Nursing Core Course (TNCC): Required within 1 year of hire or transfer into the department.(Current employees as of 06\\.30\\.2021 have 2 years to complete) Registered Nurse (RN) license in state of practice: Required Advanced Cardiac Life Support (ACLS) or Healthstream Advanced Cardiac Life Support (HS\\-ACLS) or RQIACLS: Required Essential Functions: Provides appropriate nursing interventions in stable and emergent patient situations * Is competent in all routine nursing skills including: (1\\) medication administration, (2\\) intravenous therapy including blood products, (3\\) care \\& maintenance of indwelling catheters \\& drains and, (4\\) safe use of any medical equipment used in the critical care setting, (5\\) Understands and follows chain of command per policy * Accurately interprets ECG rhythm and manages care of patients during life threatening emergencies in populations served * Verifies alarm settings with Monitor Technician according to policy * Demonstrates knowledge of lead functions and application, able to make recommendations for best monitoring based on patient presentation * Demonstrates knowledge/skill regarding the care and management of patients requiring ICP and ventriculostomy monitoring * Is proficient in assessment and intervention in changes in neurological symptoms * Demonstrates knowledge/skill regarding the care and management of patients requiring Pulmonary Artery Monitoring Catheters and patients requiring Arterial Pressure Monitoring Catheters * Demonstrates knowledge/skill regarding the care and management of patients requiring mechanical ventilation and other ventilatory support devises I.e * C\\-pap mask; Nasal bi\\-pap mask; High Flow O2 mask * Demonstrates knowledge regarding the principles of multisystem alteration and its impact on physiological functions, developmental tasks and alteration in mental processing of the adult and elder population * Initiates a multidisciplinary, goal oriented care plan for each patient in collaboration with members of the health care teamand provides care based on that plan * Educates patients and/or their families regarding care * Uses teaching methods appropriate to the age, condition, culture, and assessed learning\\-readiness of the patient * Completes legible, accurate and timely documentation of all nursing care provided, including evaluation of patient's response to medications, treatments \\& procedures * Consistently performs necessary unit specific safety \\& quality control checks (i.e * Shift and 24hr MD order checks, VO/TO Read Back, crash cart checks, urine QC, refrig logs \\& outdate checks) * Performs other job\\-related duties as assigned.
Requirements
Education
* Graduate of an accredited healthcare program related to the specialty
Licensure \\& Certifications
* Active professional license in the state of practice * Current Basic Life Support (BLS) certification
Experience
* Minimum 1–2 years of recent experience in the specialty
Pay \\& Benefits
* Competitive weekly pay packages * Comprehensive support throughout your travel assignment * Competitive compensation package with travel assignment support * Support from experienced recruiters
Travel Perks
* Assignments available nationwide * Opportunity to gain experience in different healthcare settings
Dedicated recruiter support throughout your assignment
\\#Workwolf 🔗 Apply Direct: https://app.workwolf.com/pipelineLink/5XTYJXTV?utm_source=indeed 🔗 Job Board: https://www.indeed.com/viewjob?jk=eb19b565934b94fe

📝 Description: **Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)**
Hiring a Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN) to work in a long\\-term care setting in **Geneva, New York**.
* **Contract:** 13 weeks (Contract\\-to\\-Perm option) * **Schedule:** 40 hours per week * **Shift:** Full\\-Time Evenings \\| 5x8s \\| Every Other Weekend \\& Holiday Required * **Setting:** Long Term Care * **Requirements:**
Active New York LPN License
BLS (AHA or RQI required)
Minimum 1 year of LPN experience
Experience with adult and geriatric patients * **Responsibilities:**

Ensure safe care practices, including infection control compliance * **Compensation:**

item.title: Registered Nurse (RN) at TEEMA Group
item.guid: TEEMA Group-Registered Nurse (RN)-Larimore, ND, US
item.description:
📍 Location: Larimore, ND, US 💼 Type: Fulltime 🏠 Work: On-site 💰 Salary: USD 32 - 48 (hourly)
📝 Description: **Registered Nurse (RN) – Long\\-Term Care**
**Role Summary**
The Registered Nurse (RN) plays a vital role in delivering compassionate, high\\-quality nursing care to residents in a long\\-term care setting. Using the nursing process, the RN provides individualized, patient\\-centered care while collaborating closely with residents, families, and interdisciplinary colleagues. This role offers the opportunity to practice across the full scope of professional nursing, support resident well\\-being at every stage of care, and contribute to a supportive, team\\-oriented clinical environment.
This position is ideal for nurses seeking stability, strong compensation, meaningful resident relationships, and a schedule that supports work–life balance.
**Key Duties \\& Responsibilities**
Utilize the nursing process (assessment, diagnosis, planning, implementation, and evaluation) to provide individualized resident care
Collaborate with residents, families, providers, and care team members to plan, implement, and evaluate care
Deliver nursing care across preventative care, health maintenance, diagnosis, and treatment phases
Coordinate resident care, conduct assessments, provide education, and implement appropriate nursing interventions
Practice within the full scope of nursing knowledge, utilizing age\\- and population\\-specific standards of care
Serve as Charge Nurse as assigned, delegating tasks to licensed and unlicensed assistive personnel based on competency and resident needs
Provide guidance, supervision, and support to delegated staff to ensure safe, effective care delivery
Ensure compliance with the Nurse Practice Act, administrative rules, and professional standards of nursing practice
Uphold the Scope and Standards of Professional Nursing Practice and the Code of Ethics for Nurses
**Required Qualifications**
Active, unencumbered Registered Nurse (RN) license in the state of practice
Multistate licensure accepted for Nurse Licensure Compact (NLC) states
Ability to obtain and maintain all required department\\-specific competencies and certifications
**Desired Qualifications**
Graduation from a nationally accredited nursing program (e.g., CCNE, ACEN, NLN CNEA)
Bachelor’s degree in Nursing (BSN) preferred
Experience in long\\-term care or similar clinical settings
Strong clinical judgment, communication, and teamwork skills
**Location \\& Work Type**
**Location:** Larimore, North Dakota
**Work Type:** On\\-site
**Schedule**: Full\\-time, day or night shift
**Benefits Summary**
This role offers an attractive and competitive benefits package. Benefits include medical, dental, vision, and life insurance; a 401(k) retirement plan; work–life balance programs; and a generous paid time\\-off package designed to support a healthy balance between work and personal life. Benefits availability may vary based on employment status and eligibility.
**If you're interested, please reply to this advertisement or directly email your resume to me at DSalgado@teemagroup.com or by calling/texting (949\\) 295\\-5951\\.**
**I strive to reply within 48 hours. Looking forward to connecting with you soon. Thank you!**
\\#Workwolf 🔗 Apply Direct: https://app.workwolf.com/pipelineLink/HTCJ3VRA?utm_source=indeed 🔗 Job Board: https://www.indeed.com/viewjob?jk=579f2d7a161e5385

item.description:
📍 Location: Phoenix, AZ, US 💼 Type: Temporary 🏠 Work: On-site
📝 Description: **RN – CVOR**
**Phoenix, AZ**
We’re seeking an experienced **Cardiovascular Operating Room (CVOR) Registered Nurse** to join a high\\-performing surgical team in **Phoenix, Arizona**. This is an excellent opportunity for an RN with strong cardiac OR experience looking to work in a fast\\-paced, highly specialized environment.
**Position Overview:**
* Provide perioperative nursing care for **cardiovascular and thoracic surgeries** * Scrub and/or circulate for open\\-heart and vascular procedures as required * Collaborate closely with surgeons, anesthesia, perfusionists, and OR staff * Ensure patient safety, sterile technique, and adherence to surgical protocols * Document care accurately and in compliance with regulatory standards
**Requirements:**
* Active **Arizona RN license** (compact accepted) * **BLS and ACLS** required * **2\\+ years of recent CVOR experience** * Experience with open\\-heart procedures (CABG, valve replacements, etc.) * Strong knowledge of cardiac surgical equipment and workflows
This role is ideal for a CVOR RN seeking professional growth in a major metro area with advanced cardiac programs.

item.title: Travel RN - CVICU in Lodi, CA at Talented Medical Solutions
item.guid: Talented Medical Solutions-Travel RN - CVICU in Lodi, CA-Lodi, CA, US
item.description:
📍 Location: Lodi, CA, US 💼 Type: Contract 🏠 Work: On-site 💰 Salary: USD 2,451 - 2,551 (weekly)
📝 Description: **Travel RN – CVICU \\| Lodi, CA \\| 13 Weeks**
We are seeking an experienced **CVICU Registered Nurse** for a **13\\-week travel assignment in Lodi, California**. This is a **night shift position with an Auto Offer**, ideal for nurses experienced in **post\\-operative cardiac care starting Day 1**.
**Position Details:**
* **Specialty:** RN – CVICU / Medical ICU * **Location:** Lodi * **Contract Length:** 13 Weeks * **Shift:** Night Shift (12\\-hour shifts) * **Hours:** 36–48 hours per week * **Unit Bed Count:** 10 beds * **Patient Ratio:** 2:1 * **Weekend Requirement:** 6 weekend shifts in a 6\\-week schedule * **Floating:** May float to MS/Telemetry or task in ED * **Call:** Not required
**Job Summary:**
The CVICU RN provides coordinated, high\\-quality nursing care for critically ill patients using the nursing process and established clinical standards. Responsibilities include patient assessment, care planning, intervention implementation, and evaluation of outcomes. The RN collaborates with interdisciplinary teams, supervises nursing staff, and promotes a safe and healing environment for patients and families.
**Key Responsibilities:**
* Assess patient conditions and analyze clinical data to determine care needs * Develop and implement individualized care plans * Provide post\\-operative cardiac care and critical care interventions * Collaborate with physicians and multidisciplinary teams * Educate patients and families on treatment and recovery * Participate in discharge planning and continuity of care * Delegate tasks and coordinate with healthcare team members
**Requirements:**
* Active **Registered Nurse (RN) license – State of California** * **BLS or CPR certification** required * **Post\\-operative CVICU experience required (Day 1 readiness)** * **BSN preferred** * Acute care experience preferred
If you're a **CVICU RN experienced in post\\-op cardiac care** looking for your next travel assignment, this opportunity in **Lodi, CA** could be a great fit. Submit your updated resume to p.soriano@talentedmedicalsolutions.com or call/text (520\\) 762\\-6168\\. Looking forward to connect!
\\#Workwolf 🔗 Apply Direct: https://app.workwolf.com/pipelineLink/HNZKUXAD?utm_source=indeed 🔗 Job Board: https://www.indeed.com/viewjob?jk=28146db55d325358

item.title: Local LPN opportunity at Talented Medical Solutions
item.guid: Talented Medical Solutions-Local LPN opportunity-Peterborough, NH, US
item.description:
📍 Location: Peterborough, NH, US 💼 Type: Fulltime, Contract 🏠 Work: On-site
📝 Description: **Job Summary**
Local LPN opportunity
Talented Medical Solutions
Full\\-time, Contract Day Shift
In\\-Office \\| Peterborough, NH, United States
**The Opportunity**
You’re the kind of person who is always looking to learn. You want to grow into something greater and you’re looking for an employer encourages and supports your professional development. Talented Medical Solutions promotes advancement and rewards our employees based on individual performance and merit. Sure you’ll have exposure to the team, but all of our people are accountable for their success. Competitive and focused, our team is on a mission to deliver excellence. We also know that this can only be accomplished by supporting our employee growth and development. By providing frequent feedback and consistently measuring progress, we’ve discovered the recipe for success that’s delivered year over year growth since our first year of business. This a culture of winning. At Talented Medical Solutions we breed winners.
**Responsibilities**
* Provide direct patient care and support under the supervision of registered nurses. * Administer medications and monitor patient responses to treatments. * Assist in developing and implementing patient care plans. * Participate in ongoing professional development and training opportunities. * Engage in performance measurement and strive to meet established targets. * Document patient progress and maintain accurate medical records. * Collaborate with healthcare team members to optimize patient outcomes. * Ensure compliance with healthcare regulations and safety standards. * Educate patients and families on health management and wellness. * Monitor vital signs and report any significant changes to the nursing staff. * Assist with daily living activities to enhance patient comfort. * Maintain a clean and safe environment for patients and staff.
**Experience/Qualifications**
* Current LPN licensure in the state of employment. * Commitment to ongoing professional development and training. * Experience in providing direct patient care in a clinical setting. * Ability to engage patients and families in health education. * Strong understanding of medication administration and monitoring patient responses. * Familiarity with healthcare regulations and safety standards. * Willingness to monitor vital signs and report changes promptly. * Ability to collaborate effectively with a multidisciplinary healthcare team. * Strong documentation skills for maintaining accurate patient records. * Experience in assisting with daily living activities for patient comfort.
**What's in it for you? (Salary, Commissions \\& Benefits)**
* Enjoy a collaborative and engaging work environment at our on\\-site location in beautiful Peterborough, NH. * Receive a competitive base salary that reflects your skills and contributions.
\\#Workwolf 🔗 Apply Direct: https://app.workwolf.com/pipelineLink/EHBZVVYA?utm_source=indeed 🔗 Job Board: https://www.indeed.com/viewjob?jk=ec097376eb5a712f

item.title: Travel Registered Nurse (RN) – OR Inpatient at Talented Medical Solutions
item.guid: Talented Medical Solutions-Travel Registered Nurse (RN) – OR Inpatient-Mesa, AZ, US
item.description:
📍 Location: Mesa, AZ, US 💼 Type: Contract 🏠 Work: On-site 💰 Salary: USD 2,087 - 2,187 (weekly)
📝 Description: **Registered Nurse (RN) – OR Inpatient**
**Location:** Arizona
**Specialty:** Surgical Hospital – Operating Room (Inpatient)
**Shift:** Nights \\| (3\\) 12\\-hour shifts
**Hours:** 6:40 PM – 7:10 AM
**Guaranteed Hours:** 36 hours per week
**Schedule:** 18:45 – 7:15
We are seeking an experienced **Operating Room Registered Nurse (RN)** for an inpatient surgical hospital in Arizona. This position is ideal for a skilled OR nurse who thrives in a fast\\-paced surgical environment and is comfortable working night shifts.
### **Job Description:**
The OR RN will provide direct patient care before, during, and after surgical procedures. Responsibilities include circulating cases, collaborating with surgeons and surgical teams, maintaining sterile technique, monitoring patient status, and ensuring accurate documentation. The RN will also assist with patient positioning, equipment preparation, and post\\-operative care transitions.
### **Requirements:**
* Active Arizona RN license (or compact license) * Recent OR inpatient experience required * BLS (AHA) required * Ability to take call and float per department guidelines * Must be flexible to work holidays as scheduled
This is a great opportunity to join a supportive surgical team while gaining valuable inpatient OR experience in Arizona.
Interested candidates, apply today to learn more! Submit your updated resume to p.soriano@talentedmedicalsolutions.com or call/text (520\\) 762\\-6168

item.title: LPN Charge Nurse (Per-Diem) at Sisters of Mercy of the Americas
item.guid: Sisters of Mercy of the Americas-LPN Charge Nurse (Per-Diem)-Savannah, GA, US
item.description:
📍 Location: Savannah, GA, US 💼 Type: Parttime 🏠 Work: On-site 💰 Salary: USD 24 - 30 (hourly)
📝 Description: **Job description:**
**Per Diem LPN – Savannah, GA**
**Pay Range: $24\\.00–$30\\.00 per hour (based on experience)**
**Need:** Willingness to work holidays, either weekend shift, day shift (7am\\-3pm), evening shift (3pm\\-11pm) or night shift (11pm\\-7am).
**Number of openings: 2**
Mercy Convent, home to the Sisters of Mercy, is seeking compassionate and dependable **LPN** to join our team on a **per diem/PRN** basis. This role provides direct care to the Sisters and oversees caregivers during assigned shifts to ensure high\\-quality, mission\\-aligned service.
**What You’ll Do**
* Provide direct nursing care per care plan and physician orders * Supervise and support caregivers during assigned shifts * Complete assessments, vitals, documentation, and medication administration * Respond promptly to Sisters’ needs and any changes in condition * Maintain a safe, clean environment; follow all safety and infection\\-control protocols * Communicate clearly with the DON/RCA, team members, and Sisters * Assist with admissions, transfers, incident reporting, and chart updates
**What We’re Looking For**
* **Active LPN license in Missouri** * **BLS certification** * Minimum **2 years of nursing experience** * Strong communication, organization, and leadership abilities * Ability to lift/push/pull up to 75 lbs and meet general physical demands * Ability to work both independently and as part of a team
**Why Work With Us**
* Meaningful work serving the Sisters of Mercy * Supportive, mission\\-centered environment * Flexible per diem scheduling (days/evenings/nights)
\\#Workwolf 🔗 Apply Direct: https://app.workwolf.com/pipelineLink/2ZNWDMVE?utm_source=indeed 🔗 Job Board: https://www.indeed.com/viewjob?jk=27502af0e94eff96

item.title: RN - Med/Surg at HiredFirst
item.guid: HiredFirst-RN - Med/Surg-Fort Bragg, CA, US
item.description:
📍 Location: Fort Bragg, CA, US 💼 Type: Fulltime, Contract 🏠 Work: On-site 💰 Salary: USD 57 - 60 (hourly)
📝 Description: Fort Bragg, California
13\\-Week Travel Contract
Assignment: April 13, 2026 – July 12, 2026
Night Shift \\| 7:00 PM – 7:30 AM \\| 36 Hours per Week
A healthcare facility located on the Northern California coast is seeking an experienced Medical\\-Surgical Registered Nurse (RN) for a 13\\-week travel assignment in Fort Bragg, CA. This position provides care to a diverse patient population in a general acute care unit supporting both medical and surgical patients.
This opportunity is ideal for nurses who thrive in a collaborative environment and are comfortable managing a wide variety of patient conditions.
**Position Details** --------------------
* Schedule: 36 hours per week * Shift: Night shift \\| 12\\-hour shifts (7:00 PM – 7:30 AM) * Contract Length: 13 weeks * Department: Medical\\-Surgical Acute Care * Patient Population: Mixed medical and surgical patients
**Responsibilities** --------------------
* Provide comprehensive nursing care to medical and surgical patients * Monitor patient conditions and respond to changes in health status * Develop and implement individualized patient care plans * Administer medications and treatments as ordered * Collaborate with physicians and interdisciplinary healthcare teams * Educate patients and families regarding treatment plans and discharge instructions * Maintain accurate clinical documentation in accordance with facility guidelines * Assist with discharge planning and continuity of care
**Required Qualifications** ---------------------------
* Active Registered Nurse (RN) license * BLS certification required * ACLS certification required * Telemetry experience required * Acute care hospital experience
### **Preferred**
* Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN)
**Location** ------------
Fort Bragg is located along the beautiful Mendocino Coast of Northern California, offering dramatic ocean views, towering redwood forests, and outdoor recreation opportunities. The area is known for whale watching, hiking, coastal trails, and scenic beaches, providing an incredible setting for travel healthcare professionals.

item.title: Director of Nursing at TEEMA
item.guid: TEEMA-Director of Nursing-Larimore, ND, US
item.description:
📍 Location: Larimore, ND, US 💼 Type: Fulltime 🏠 Work: On-site 💰 Salary: USD 45 - 58 (hourly)
📝 Description: **Job Summary**
Director of Nursing
TEEMA Staffing
Full\\-time
In\\-Office \\| Larimore, ND, United States
**The Opportunity**
You’re the kind of person who is always looking to learn. You want to grow into something greater and you’re looking for an employer encourages and supports your professional development. TEEMA Staffing promotes advancement and rewards our employees based on individual performance and merit. Sure you’ll have exposure to the team, but all of our people are accountable for their success. Competitive and focused, our team is on a mission to deliver excellence. We also know that this can only be accomplished by supporting our employee growth and development. By providing frequent feedback and consistently measuring progress, we’ve discovered the recipe for success that’s delivered year over year growth since our first year of business. This a culture of winning. At TEEMA Staffing we breed winners.
**Responsibilities**
* Oversee nursing staff and ensure high\\-quality patient care standards. * Monitor and evaluate nursing performance and patient outcomes regularly. * Establish clear performance targets and monitor progress towards goals. * Develop and implement nursing policies and procedures for best practices. * Collaborate with healthcare teams to enhance patient care strategies. * Ensure compliance with healthcare regulations and standards. * Lead initiatives to improve patient satisfaction and care quality. * Foster a culture of continuous learning and professional development. * Conduct regular training sessions to improve nursing skills and knowledge. * Analyze data to track performance metrics and identify improvement areas. * Manage budgets and resources effectively to meet departmental goals. * Encourage open communication and feedback within the nursing team.
**Experience/Qualifications**
* Proven experience in nursing leadership roles, preferably at a director level. * Commitment to continuous professional development and staff training. * Strong understanding of healthcare regulations and compliance standards. * Experience in performance evaluation and data analysis for nursing outcomes. * Proven track record of improving patient satisfaction and care quality. * Demonstrated ability to develop and implement nursing policies effectively. * Ability to manage budgets and resources to meet departmental goals. * Experience in setting performance targets and monitoring progress. * Strong communication skills to foster team collaboration and feedback. * Ability to analyze performance metrics to identify improvement areas.
**What's in it for you? (Salary, Commissions \\& Benefits)**
* Enjoy a collaborative work environment with on\\-site opportunities in Larimore, ND.
\\#Workwolf \\#talentsolutions 🔗 Apply Direct: https://app.workwolf.com/pipelineLink/QLLTMEQS?utm_source=indeed 🔗 Job Board: https://www.indeed.com/viewjob?jk=510d92a6bbd9102f

item.title: RN – Labor & Delivery Clearlake, CA at Talented Medical Solutions
item.guid: Talented Medical Solutions-RN – Labor & Delivery Clearlake, CA-Clearlake, CA, US
item.description:
📍 Location: Clearlake, CA, US 💼 Type: Temporary 🏠 Work: On-site 💰 Salary: USD 3,100 - 3,200 (weekly)
📝 Description: **RN – Labor \\& Delivery**
**Clearlake, CA**
We’re seeking an experienced **L\\&D RN** for primarily **night shifts** in **Clearlake, California**. This role is ideal for nurses with strong labor, delivery, and postpartum experience who are comfortable supporting occasional day shift coverage.
### **Assignment Details**
* **Shift:** Primarily Nights, 1800 – 0630 (some day coverage as needed) * **Hours:** 36 hours/week * **Location:** Clearlake, CA * **Experience:** 3–4 years in labor and delivery
### **Requirements**
* **Registered Nurse (RN) License – California** – Required * **BLS Certification** – Required * **ACLS Certification** – Preferred * **NRP Certification** – Required * **STABLE Certification** – Preferred * **AWHONN Fetal Monitoring Certification** – Must obtain within 6 months; recertify every 2 years
### **Experience \\& Skills**
* Strong labor \\& delivery experience * Comfortable with **postpartum care** * Nursery experience (any level) appreciated * Familiarity with hospital crash cart, patient monitoring, and emergency protocols * Ability to collaborate with interdisciplinary teams
### **Key Responsibilities**
* Provide comprehensive nursing care in labor, delivery, and postpartum units * Monitor fetal and maternal status using appropriate techniques and equipment * Assist with deliveries and perform postpartum assessments * Document patient care accurately and timely * Respond to emergencies per hospital policies and protocols * Support team coverage on occasional day shifts as needed
This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced **L\\&D RN** to work primarily night shifts in a supportive environment in **Clearlake, CA**.
\\#Workwolf 🔗 Apply Direct: https://app.workwolf.com/pipelineLink/5FIGB0AA?utm_source=indeed 🔗 Job Board: https://www.indeed.com/viewjob?jk=1c477d6a1e889903

item.title: Travel RN – Med/Surg in Lodi, CA at Talented Medical Solutions
item.guid: Talented Medical Solutions-Travel RN – Med/Surg in Lodi, CA-Lodi, CA, US
item.description:
📍 Location: Lodi, CA, US 💼 Type: Contract 🏠 Work: On-site 💰 Salary: USD 1,989 - 2,089 (weekly)
📝 Description: **RN – Med/Surg \\| Acute Care \\| 13 Weeks \\| Night Shift**
We are seeking a **Registered Nurse – Med/Surg** for a 13\\-week travel assignment in Lodi. This night\\-shift role offers **36–48 hours per week** with the opportunity to work in a dynamic acute care environment supporting Med/Surg and Telemetry patients.
**Position Details:**
* **Specialty:** RN – Med/Surg Acute * **Shift:** Night Shift (3rd 12s) * **Hours:** 36–48 per week * **Contract Length:** 13 Weeks * **Unit Beds:** 22 beds (Med/Surg \\& Tele capable) * **Ratios:** Telemetry 4:1 \\| Med/Surg 5:1 * **Floating:** Required per union contract to Med/Surg/Tele and other departments as needed * **Call:** Required for permanent staff * **Weekend Rotation:** Every other weekend (subject to operational needs)
**Job Summary:**
The Med/Surg RN delivers coordinated nursing care following established standards and the nursing process. Responsibilities include patient assessment, care planning, implementation, and evaluation of outcomes. The RN collaborates with interdisciplinary teams, supervises nursing activities, and promotes safe, patient\\-centered care using critical thinking and professional judgment.
**Key Responsibilities:**
* Collect and analyze patient data to guide clinical decisions * Develop and implement individualized care plans * Provide education to patients and families * Coordinate care with healthcare teams * Participate in discharge planning * Delegate tasks and supervise support staff * Float and assist in other units as required
**Requirements:**
* Active RN license in the state of practice * BLS/CPR certification (required) * Acute care experience preferred * BSN preferred
**Facility Support:**
* Pyxis medication system * Cerner documentation * 24/7 pharmacy and hospitalist coverage * IV team (day shift availability) * Phlebotomy services available
If you are a dedicated Med/Surg RN seeking a travel opportunity in a supportive acute care setting, we would love to connect. Travelers must be comfortable floating and providing care across Med/Surg and Telemetry units as part of the assignment. Submit your updated resume to p.soriano@talentedmedicalsolutions.com or call/text (520\\) 762\\-6168
\\#Workwolf 🔗 Apply Direct: https://app.workwolf.com/pipelineLink/NKUJL0WN?utm_source=indeed 🔗 Job Board: https://www.indeed.com/viewjob?jk=14eb102943a06b5e

item.title: RN - PACU (Post-Anesthesia Care Unit) at HiredFirst
item.guid: HiredFirst-RN - PACU (Post-Anesthesia Care Unit)-Clearlake, CA, US
item.description:
📍 Location: Clearlake, CA, US 💼 Type: Fulltime, Contract 🏠 Work: On-site 💰 Salary: USD 60 - 63 (hourly)
📝 Description: 13\\-Week Travel Contract
Assignment: March 30, 2026 – June 28, 2026
Day Shift \\| 4 x 10s \\| 7:00 AM – 5:30 PM
A healthcare facility in Northern California is seeking an experienced PACU Registered Nurse (RN) for a 13\\-week travel assignment in Clearlake, CA. This role involves providing post\\-anesthesia care for surgical patients while collaborating with interdisciplinary teams to ensure safe recovery and optimal patient outcomes.
This opportunity is ideal for nurses with strong post\\-operative monitoring and recovery experience who thrive in a fast\\-paced surgical environment.
**Position Details** --------------------
* Schedule: Monday – Thursday * Shift: Day shift \\| 4 x 10\\-hour shifts * Hours: 7:00 AM – 5:30 PM * Contract Length: 13 weeks * On\\-Call Requirement: Every third weekend * Setting: PACU / Surgical Services
**Responsibilities** --------------------
* Provide comprehensive nursing care to patients recovering from anesthesia and surgical procedures * Monitor patient vital signs, airway status, and overall recovery from anesthesia * Identify complications and intervene appropriately using clinical judgment * Collaborate with surgeons, anesthesiologists, and interdisciplinary care teams * Administer medications and treatments as ordered * Educate patients and families on post\\-procedure care and recovery expectations * Document patient care accurately and in accordance with facility policies * Assist with discharge planning to ensure continuity of care
**Required Qualifications** ---------------------------
* Active Registered Nurse (RN) license * Minimum 3 years of PACU or acute care experience * BLS certification required * ACLS certification required * PALS certification required
### **Preferred**
* Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) * Acute care hospital experience
**Location** ------------
Clearlake, California is located in Lake County in Northern California, offering beautiful lake views and outdoor recreation opportunities. The area is known for fishing, hiking, biking, and a relaxed lifestyle while still being within driving distance of San Francisco, Sacramento, and the Pacific Coast.

item.guid: TEEMA Group-Registered Nurse - RN-Inglewood, CA, US
item.description:
📍 Location: Inglewood, CA, US 💼 Type: Fulltime 🏠 Work: On-site 💰 Salary: USD 40 - 50 (hourly)
📝 Description: **Job Summary:** This is an exciting opportunity to make a meaningful impact by delivering high\\-quality, patient\\-centered care in a thriving clinical environment. The ideal candidate will bring clinical expertise, a passion for service, and a commitment to excellence in patient outcomes.
**Key Responsibilities:**
* Deliver comprehensive, high\\-quality nursing care under the supervision of the Nursing Supervisor, Nursing Director, or Chief Nursing Executive. * Assume full accountability for the nursing process, ensuring efficient, compassionate, and evidence\\-based care for patients. * Collaborate closely with physicians, clinical operations, and team members to support the achievement of patient care and clinical goals. * Conducted nursing evaluations and implemented appropriate nursing interventions based on clinical assessment. * Maintain accurate and timely documentation in compliance with regulatory standards and internal protocols. * Contribute to the development and implementation of efficient, standardized systems and protocols to improve the quality of care across clinics. * Provide clinical support in various care areas, including urgent care triage, nurse clinics, and clinic floor operations as needed. * Demonstrate clinical competence for the specific patient population served, adhering to professional, ethical, and organizational standards. * Other duties as assigned.
**Qualifications:**
* Graduate of an accredited nursing program with a current California Registered Nurse (RN) license. * Minimum of one (1\\) year of experience as a Registered Nurse. * Current Basic Life Support (BLS) certification. * Strong documentation and communication skills with attention to detail. * A commitment to culturally sensitive and patient\\-focused care.
**Desired Qualifications:**
* At least 1 year of experience working with elderly or medically complex populations. * Experience in an ambulatory care setting such as a clinic or urgent care center. * Current Advanced Cardiac Life Support (ACLS) certification. * Bilingual proficiency in English and Spanish, Mandarin, or Cantonese, depending on regional demographics.
**Benefits \\& Career Growth**
* Medical, Dental and Vision insurance, * Retirement savings plans with employer matching contributions * Flexible Spending Accounts * Commuter Flexible Spending * Career Advancement \\& Development opportunities * Paid Time Off \\& Holidays * Paid CME Days * Malpractice insurance and tail coverage * Tuition Reimbursement Program * Corporate Employee Discounts * Employee Referral Bonus Program * Pet Care Insurance * and more!
**Setting**: Outpatient Clinic working with the adults and geriatric population
This is an incredible opportunity for an RN who is passionate about making a meaningful impact in patients' lives while working in a dynamic and supportive team environment. If you are eager to bring your expertise and compassion to a growing healthcare organization, we encourage you to apply!

13\\-Week Travel Contract

Day Shift \\| 36 Hours per Week
A healthcare facility on the Northern California coast is seeking an experienced Emergency Room Registered Nurse (RN) for a 13\\-week travel assignment in Fort Bragg, CA. This role focuses on delivering high\\-quality emergency care to patients of all ages in a busy acute care emergency department.
**Position Details** --------------------
* Schedule: 36 hours per week * Shift: Day shift \\| 12\\-hour shifts * Contract Length: 13 weeks * Department: Emergency Department * Patient Population: Trauma and emergency patients of all ages

* Triage patients and prioritize care in emergency situations * Provide comprehensive nursing care for trauma and acute conditions * Assess patient conditions and implement appropriate treatment plans * Administer medications and emergency interventions * Collaborate with physicians, specialists, and healthcare team members * Document patient care and treatment plans accurately * Support patient education and discharge planning
**Required Qualifications** ---------------------------
* Active Registered Nurse (RN) license * BLS certification required * ACLS certification required * PALS certification required * NRP certification required * Emergency Department experience required
### **Preferred**
* TNCC certification

item.title: Director of Nursing at TEEMA Group
item.guid: TEEMA Group-Director of Nursing-Larimore, ND, US
item.description:
📍 Location: Larimore, ND, US 💼 Type: Fulltime 🏠 Work: On-site 💰 Salary: USD 48 - 58 (hourly)
📝 Description: **Director of Nursing (DON) – Long\\-Term Care**
**Role Summary**
The Director of Nursing (DON) provides strategic and operational leadership for nursing services within a long\\-term care setting. This role is responsible for ensuring the delivery of high\\-quality, resident\\-centered care while maintaining compliance with all regulatory, professional, and organizational standards. The DON leads nursing personnel, oversees daily operations, and partners with interdisciplinary leadership to promote clinical excellence, operational efficiency, and continuous quality improvement.
This is an outstanding opportunity for a seasoned nursing leader who is passionate about geriatric care, team development, and driving positive outcomes in a dynamic healthcare environment.
**Key Duties \\& Responsibilities**
Administer and oversee the nursing program to maintain high standards of resident care
Ensure the overall quality, safety, and effectiveness of nursing services provided by clinical staff
Lead day\\-to\\-day operations of the long\\-term care facility, including care delivery, staffing, environmental safety, budget oversight, and regulatory compliance
Monitor nursing operations and ensure adherence to organizational policies, professional standards, and governmental regulations
Schedule nursing staff, conduct performance evaluations, and support staff development
Provide direct nursing care to residents as needed
Advise medical staff, department leaders, and administrators on nursing services, clinical strategies, and care initiatives
Interpret and communicate nursing policies and objectives to staff and community groups
Lead the development and execution of clinical programs, policies, procedures, and protocols
Coordinate and delegate clinical, operational, and managerial activities to meet departmental and organizational goals
Foster effective communication across teams to adapt to the evolving healthcare environment
Assist in the development and implementation of marketing and community outreach activities, including special events and engagement initiatives
Promote system integration and consistency across clinical programs and services
**Required Qualifications**
Bachelor’s degree in Nursing (BSN)
Minimum of five (5\\) years of nursing experience
Current, unencumbered Registered Nurse (RN) license in the state of practice
Multistate licensure accepted if practicing in a Nurse Licensure Compact (NLC) state
Thorough knowledge of nursing practice standards, nursing regulations, and long\\-term care operations
Strong leadership, administrative, and management skills
Ability to obtain and maintain required department\\-specific competencies and certifications
**Desired Qualifications**
Graduation from a nationally accredited nursing program (e.g., CCNE, ACEN, NLN CNEA)
Experience in long\\-term care, assisted living, housing with services, or HUD\\-supported housing environments
Demonstrated success leading clinical teams in rural or community\\-based healthcare settings
Strong communication, organizational, and change\\-management skills
Note: In select rural or community\\-based settings, an approved educational development plan may be considered for qualified candidates actively pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Nursing.
**Location \\& Work Type**
**Location:** Larimore, North Dakota
**Work Type:** On\\-site
**Schedule**: Full\\-time, day shift
**Benefits Summary**
This role offers an attractive and competitive benefits package. Benefits include medical, dental, vision, and life insurance; a 401(k) retirement plan; work–life balance programs; and a generous paid time\\-off package designed to support a healthy balance between work and personal life. Benefits availability may vary based on employment status and eligibility.
**If you're interested, please reply to this advertisement or directly email your resume to me at DSalgado@teemagroup.com or by calling/texting (949\\) 295\\-5951\\.**
**I strive to reply within 48 hours. Looking forward to connecting with you soon. Thank you!**
\\#Workwolf 🔗 Apply Direct: https://app.workwolf.com/pipelineLink/ZVMRRT1I?utm_source=indeed 🔗 Job Board: https://www.indeed.com/viewjob?jk=f716ee19fb368b9c

item.title: Registered Nurse - NICU at HiredFirst
item.guid: HiredFirst-Registered Nurse - NICU-Bakersfield, CA, US
item.description:
📍 Location: Bakersfield, CA, US 💼 Type: Fulltime, Contract 🏠 Work: On-site 💰 Salary: USD 60 - 63 (hourly)
📝 Description: **Position Overview** ---------------------
A healthcare facility in Bakersfield, California is seeking an experienced Neonatal Intensive Care Unit (NICU) Registered Nurse (RN) for a 13\\-week assignment. The NICU RN will provide specialized care for critically ill and premature newborns while working collaboratively with neonatologists and multidisciplinary care teams.
This role requires strong neonatal critical care skills, the ability to work in a fast\\-paced environment, and a commitment to delivering compassionate care to infants and their families.
Cerner EMR experience is required for this assignment.
**Facility Overview** ---------------------
This facility includes a 254\\-bed acute care hospital and more than 20 primary and specialty care medical offices, delivering high\\-quality care to the Bakersfield community and surrounding regions.
Bakersfield offers affordable West Coast living, excellent weather, and convenient access to many of California’s most popular destinations, including:
* Yosemite National Park * Sequoia National Park * Central California Coast * Southern California sports, entertainment, and attractions
**Unit Information** --------------------
The Neonatal Intensive Care Unit (NICU) provides advanced care for critically ill or premature newborns requiring intensive monitoring and specialized treatment.
The unit is designated as a Level III or Level IV NICU, according to the American Academy of Pediatrics (AAP), and treats infants with life\\-threatening conditions requiring advanced respiratory support, surgical care, and continuous monitoring.
**Schedule Details** --------------------
* Shift: Night Shift * Hours: 36 hours per week (3x12\\-hour shifts) * Contract Length: 13 weeks
**Key Responsibilities** ------------------------
* Assess and monitor neonatal patients with complex medical conditions * Develop and implement individualized care plans using the nursing process * Provide specialized NICU nursing care in accordance with established standards of practice * Collaborate with neonatologists, pediatric specialists, and interdisciplinary teams * Educate and support parents and families regarding infant care and treatment plans * Monitor patient progress and adjust care plans accordingly * Participate in discharge planning to ensure continuity of care * Delegate responsibilities and coordinate tasks among healthcare team members * Maintain accurate patient documentation and clinical records * Perform additional duties as assigned
**Minimum Requirements** ------------------------
Education
* Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) – Preferred
Experience
* Acute care hospital experience – Preferred * NICU experience strongly preferred * Cerner EMR experience – Required
Licensure \\& Certifications
* Active California Registered Nurse (RN) License – Required * BLS / CPR Certification – Required
\\#Workwolf 🔗 Apply Direct: https://app.workwolf.com/pipelineLink/3YMC0F3E?utm_source=indeed 🔗 Job Board: https://www.indeed.com/viewjob?jk=197b3ac18679df1c

Also, **floating requirements must be PCU**; may float to other MS or PCU units and take unit appropriate assignments; must have **2 years of PCU experience** and prior **travel experience** to be considered. * **IMPORTANT:** This job is for TRAVELERS ONLY and will not accept any Arizona residents.
Please apply today to discuss further!

item.title: Assistant Nurse Manager OR at TEEMA
item.guid: TEEMA-Assistant Nurse Manager OR-White Plains, NY, US
item.description:
📍 Location: White Plains, NY, US 💼 Type: Fulltime 🏠 Work: On-site 💰 Salary: USD 130,000 - 165,000 (yearly)
📝 Description: **Job Summary**
Assistant Nurse Manager OR
Permanent Position
Salary $130,000 to $165,000 DOE
Excellent benefits
Our client is looking for an experienced OR RN that has leadership experience. This is a 280 bed Hospital with substantial growth. This Hospital is ranked as one of the best Hospitals by Money Magazine.
**Here is what you will do:**
Assures the delivery of quality department /clinical services in accordance with established hospital and regulatory/accreditation agency standards.
Promotes a positive and professional image and is a role model, coach, mentor, problem solver and resource for staff and peers.
Responsible and accountable for the overall operations of their assigned department. This includes and is not limited to: development of department goals and objectives that are consistent with the hospital mission and strategic plan, the management of personnel, preparing/monitoring of and accountability for the department budgets.
Responsible for management of FTE component of staffing budgets
Collaborates with physicians and other members of the Health Care Team to assure the delivery of optimum age appropriate patient care
Provides hospital supervision as required by the Division of Nursing
Participates in the development of Evidence based Standards of care for clinical area of responsibility
Assures employee compliance with hospital and Human Resources policies and procedures
Assures that employee competency and education requirements are satisfied as per hospital policy.
Participates in the Performance Improvement Program as defined by the organization and the Division of Nursing
Assures the provision of a safe employee/patient environment.
Maintains knowledge related to the operations of their department.
Works collaboratively with all levels of the hospital interdisciplinary team and promotes the team concept within their department and hospital wide.
Demonstrates positive customer service, fosters positive employee relations and assures that staff adheres to the Customer Service Behavioral Standards.
Is supportive of hospital initiatives and projects and functions as a positive change agent.
Develops and submits departmental goals and objectives that are aligned with the Nursing and hospital strategic plans as required by the Division Administrator.
Conducts Interviews, hires, evaluates, counsels and terminates employees as per hospital policy.
Schedules staff based on budgeted staffing plan and department needs.
Assures compliance with governmental, state regulations and accreditation standards.
Periodically provides direct patient care as needed.
Participates and assures department involvement in ongoing Performance Improvement initiatives to support quality metrics such as core measures and patient satisfaction as required.
Ensures service excellence initiatives and achieves organizational goals for patient satisfaction.
Conducts staff meetings monthly to update staff and/or address department issues and records meeting minutes for all staff.
Adheres to the tenets of the Joint Commission National Patient Safety Goals
Delegates activities to staff according to the tenets of the NYS Nurse Practice Act
Adheres to the ANA Code of Nursing Ethics
Performs all other related duties as assigned.
**Here is what you need**
Education \\& Experience Requirements
Current NY State License
BSN required
BLS certification (through American Heart Association) required
ACLS certification (through American Heart Association) preferred unless required by employees in the depAssistant Nurse Manager OR
\\#Workwolf \\#talentsolutions 🔗 Apply Direct: https://app.workwolf.com/pipelineLink/OPETKNSF?utm_source=indeed 🔗 Job Board: https://www.indeed.com/viewjob?jk=ac612df0173e3b71
